title: Naradmuni Thulung

infobox.value: This article about a Nepalese politician is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Naradmuni_Thulung&action=edit).

([Nepali](/wiki/Nepali_language "Nepali language"): नारदमुनी थुलुङ) was a [Nepalese](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal") politician, belonging to the [Nepali Congress](/wiki/Nepali_Congress "Nepali Congress"). He was an anti-Rana democratic politician. He took charge of the *Bhojpur Front* during the [1951 democratic revolution of Nepal](/wiki/Revolution_of_1951 "Revolution of 1951").[[1]](#cite_note-Tuladhar-1) In November 1951 he became Minister of Local Self-Development in the cabinet of [Matrika Prasad Koirala](/wiki/Matrika_Prasad_Koirala "Matrika Prasad Koirala"). He held the position until August 1952.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He also served as Defence minister and acted as Prime Minister during Matrika P Koirala's premiership.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Thulung became the chairman of the Royal Land Reform Commission. Under Thulung's chairmanship the commission suggested introduction of land limits, tenancy and agriculture loans. These reforms were, however, not implemented.[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was born on Chaitra 27, 1965 Vikram Samvat and died on Magh 22, 2047 Vikram Samvat (8 April 1909 to 5 February 1991 AD).[[4]](#cite_note-4)
Tuladhar, Daman Raj (1980). *Contemporary Nepal, 1945-1955*. Kathmandu: Laxmi Publication. p. 340.

["Archived copy"](https://web.archive.org/web/20080819195227/http://www.healthnet.org.np/users/dixit/ANNEX-III.PDF) (PDF). Archived from [the original](http://www.healthnet.org.np/users/dixit/ANNEX-III.PDF) (PDF) on 2008-08-19. Retrieved 2008-07-26.`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: archived copy as title ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_archived_copy_as_title "Category:CS1 maint: archived copy as title"))

["Archived copy"](https://web.archive.org/web/20080908114412/http://www2.army.mod.uk/linkedfiles/bgn/reset_brief/bgn_reset_brief_land_tenancypdf.pdf) (PDF). Archived from [the original](http://www2.army.mod.uk/linkedfiles/bgn/reset_brief/bgn_reset_brief_land_tenancypdf.pdf) (PDF) on 2008-09-08. Retrieved 2008-07-26.`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: archived copy as title ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_archived_copy_as_title "Category:CS1 maint: archived copy as title"))
Blast Khabar (2016). ["प्रथम खम्बू मन्त्री नारदमुनि थुलुङ"](https://www.blastkhabar.com/news/2016/11/18/19508.html). Blast Media, Dharan. Retrieved 18 November 2022.
Thulung, Naradmuni (1985). *Kiratko Nalibeli*. Biratnagar, Nepal: Srimati Angur Kandangwa. p. 224.

He was an anti-Rana democratic politician. He took charge of the *Bhojpur Front* during the [1951 democratic revolution of Nepal](/wiki/Revolution_of_1951 "Revolution of 1951").[[1]](#cite_note-Tuladhar-1) In November 1951 he became Minister of Local Self-Development in the cabinet of [Matrika Prasad Koirala](/wiki/Matrika_Prasad_Koirala "Matrika Prasad Koirala"). He held the position until August 1952.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He also served as Defence minister and acted as Prime Minister during Matrika P Koirala's premiership.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Thulung became the chairman of the Royal Land Reform Commission. Under Thulung's chairmanship the commission suggested introduction of land limits, tenancy and agriculture loans. **Naradmuni Thulung** ([Nepali](/wiki/Nepali_language "Nepali language"): नारदमुनी थुलुङ) was a [Nepalese](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal") politician, belonging to the [Nepali Congress](/wiki/Nepali_Congress "Nepali Congress"). He was an anti-Rana democratic politician. He took charge of the *Bhojpur Front* during the [1951 democratic revolution of Nepal](/wiki/Revolution_of_1951 "Revolution of 1951").[[1]](#cite_note-Tuladhar-1) In November 1951 he became Minister of Local Self-Development in the cabinet of [Matrika Prasad Koirala](/wiki/Matrika_Prasad_Koirala "Matrika Prasad Koirala"). He held the position until August 1952.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He also served as Defence minister and acted as Prime Minister during Matrika P Koirala's premiership.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Thulung became the chairman of the Royal Land Reform Commission. Under Thulung's chairmanship the commission suggested introduction of land limits, tenancy and agriculture loans. These reforms were, however, not implemented.[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was born on Chaitra 27, 1965 Vikram Samvat and died on Magh 22, 2047 Vikram Samvat (8 April 1909 to 5 February 1991 AD).[[4]](#cite_note-4)
